title: Shipped Dogfooding Tier Removal
goal: Implement #477 by removing repo-local maintainer/dogfooding concepts from shipped feature-tier metadata while preserving repo-local dogfooding for this source checkout.
non_goals: Do not remove repo-local tools or skills from this repository.
Do not remove reusable diagnostics that have target-repo value.
Do not redesign the whole module registry in this slice.

intent.outcome: Ordinary host-repo startup/module metadata no longer advertises maintainer dogfooding as a shipped feature tier.
intent.constraints: Keep shipped package repo/tool/language agnostic; keep dogfooding source-checkout-only.
intent.latitude: Adjust registry metadata, config/docs/tests, and generated adapter checks as needed.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the change requires removing source-checkout dogfooding tools or breaking repo-local workflow obligations.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit, push, close #477, refresh external evidence, and continue the next prioritized lane.
execution.proof: workspace CLI tests, registry/output tests, planning surface checks, and proof selector.

invariants: Source checkout dogfooding remains available through tools/ and repo config.
Ordinary host repos do not see dogfooding/self-improvement/codegen/extraction as shipped feature tiers.
Advanced reusable diagnostics remain opt-in when externally valuable.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #477 remove repo-local maintainer and dogfooding surfaces from shipped feature tiers

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Later shipped-surface and module-contract work can distinguish reusable diagnostics from source-checkout-only maintainer machinery.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #478 lifecycle, #485 module-profile redesign, #491 durable residue routing.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The package still benefits from source-checkout-only diagnostics, but those diagnostics should not appear as installable feature tiers for host repositories.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #479 lifecycle front door unless new blockers emerge.

context_budget.deep_context_loaded: Only compact package workflow surfaces and targeted changed files were used.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Dogfood package surfaces; commit/push milestone; close issue only after proof.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact metadata names for shipped reusable diagnostics versus source-checkout-only notes.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A change would remove repo-local dogfooding tools instead of removing only shipped feature-tier exposure.

active_milestone.scope: registry, output, tests, and planning state

immediate_next_action: Archive, commit, push, close #477, refresh evidence, and continue the next prioritized lane.

validation_commands.result: pass: shipped tiers exclude maintainer-dogfooding; advanced features are review_artifacts and external_adapters

uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
validation_commands.result: pass: surfaced need to record package workflow evidence before archive

pass: remaining references are tests guarding absence, explicit source-checkout command-generation docs/tests, and the reusable maintenance_pressure report section; no shipped feature tier/config-schema references remain.

completion_criteria: module_registry.json no longer contains a maintainer-dogfooding feature tier.
Advanced feature metadata no longer points at maintainer-dogfooding as a shipped tier.
Ordinary startup/report/module output does not advertise dogfooding as a shipped tier.
Repo-local dogfooding remains available through source-checkout tools and config.

execution_run.handoff source: GitHub issue #477 plus agentic-workspace start/external-intent refresh
execution_run.what happened: Removed maintainer-dogfooding as a shipped feature tier, constrained advanced feature configuration to reusable diagnostics, filtered startup skill routing by enabled shipped features, and updated docs/tests to preserve the source-checkout-only dogfooding boundary.
execution_run.scope touched: Registry metadata, config/schema validation, CLI startup/report/module output, targeted docs, tests, and planning state.
execution_run.changed surfaces: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/module_registry.json; src/agentic_workspace/config.py; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/workspace_config.schema.json; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; tests/test_workspace_cli.py; README.md; docs/contributor-playbook.md; .agentic-workspace/docs/workspace-config-contract.md; .agentic-workspace/config.toml; planning state.
execution_run.validations run: pytest workspace CLI/package tests, ruff, proof selector lanes, maintainer-surfaces, planning-surface check, modules output check, doctor, preflight, summary, workflow_obligations, closeout_trust, reconcile.
execution_run.result for continuation: #477 satisfied; continue #478 lifecycle lane, with #479 as the suggested first child.
execution_run.next step: Archive, commit, push, close #477, refresh evidence, and continue the next prioritized lane.

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: pytest workspace CLI/package tests, ruff, proof selector lanes, maintainer-surfaces, planning-surface check, modules output check, doctor, preflight, summary, workflow_obligations, closeout_trust, reconcile.

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; source-checkout diagnostics remain present but no longer define shipped tiers.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: No required follow-on for #477; continue separately tracked lifecycle, module-boundary, and residue-routing lanes.
